raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need to read file list, sum sizes. Use xargs -0? files.txt likely newline separated. Use while read -r f; do stat -c %s "$f"; done | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'. One line: awk? Use cat and while? Single line: awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum}'? No need. Use: awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum}' < <(while read -r f; do stat -c %s "$f"; done). But one line: awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum}' <<< "$(while read -r f; do stat -c %s "$f"; done < /workspace/files.txt)". Simpler: awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum}' <<< "$(stat -c %s $(cat /workspace/files.txt))". But if many files, shell expansion. Use xargs: stat -c %s $(cat /workspace/files.txt) | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'. One line: awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}' <<< "$(stat -c %s $(cat /workspace/files.txt))". I'll output: awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}' <<< "$(stat -c %s $(cat /workspace/files.txt))".
